destination.name: Beaujolais
destination.hero_image_credit: Photo by Jameson Fink on Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 2.0
destination.hero_image_alt: Morgon Cote du Py vineyard slopes in Beaujolais with village in the distance
destination.blurb: The home of Gamay: ten granite crus from the perfumed queen of Fleurie to the age-worthy king of Moulin-a-Vent, reinvented by a Gang of Four natural-wine movement that sparked a global revolution.
destination.tagline: Ten granite crus, one grape, a natural-wine revolution
destination.overview: Beaujolais stretches 55 kilometres from the Maconnais border to Lyon's outskirts, with Gamay Noir across twelve AOCs from the light Beaujolais regional tier through Beaujolais-Villages to ten named crus. The Gang of Four of Morgon sparked a global natural-wine movement.

index.title: Beaujolais Wine Region Guide | Cork & Curve
index.description: Explore Beaujolais wine estates, the ten crus and natural-wine producers from Morgon and Fleurie to Moulin-a-Vent and Cote de Brouilly. Complete Gamay country guide.

vineyards.title: Beaujolais Vineyards and Estates | Cork & Curve
vineyards.description: Discover Beaujolais estates from Marcel Lapierre and Jean Foillard to Chateau Thivin, Domaine Chignard and the natural-wine Gang of Four of Morgon.

neighborhoods.title: Beaujolais Crus and Sub-Appellations | Cork & Curve
neighborhoods.description: Beaujolais wine areas from the ten crus of Morgon, Fleurie, Moulin-a-Vent and Chiroubles to Beaujolais-Villages and the Cote du Py natural-wine heartland.

signature-grapes.title: Beaujolais Signature Grapes | Cork & Curve
signature-grapes.description: Gamay Noir and the grape varieties of Beaujolais, from the dominant Gamay crus to Chardonnay for Beaujolais Blanc and the rare white varieties of the region.

wines.title: Beaujolais Wine Catalog | Cork & Curve
wines.description: Browse Beaujolais cuvees from natural Morgon and Fleurie to Moulin-a-Vent, Beaujolais Nouveau and Cote de Brouilly. Taste profiles, pairings and producer details.

tasting-rooms.title: Beaujolais Tasting Rooms | Cork & Curve
tasting-rooms.description: Best Beaujolais tasting rooms and cellar doors from Morgon and Fleurie to Moulin-a-Vent and Brouilly. Visit the natural-wine pioneers and cru estates direct.

wine-bars.title: Beaujolais Wine Bars | Cork & Curve
wine-bars.description: Sommelier-led wine bars in Beaujolais and Lyon pouring natural Morgon, Fleurie, Moulin-a-Vent and the cru wines of Gamay country by the glass.

wine-restaurants.title: Beaujolais Wine Restaurants | Cork & Curve
wine-restaurants.description: Restaurants with serious Beaujolais wine programs, from estate kitchen tables in Morgon and Fleurie to Lyon bouchon tables pouring natural Gamay.

wine-museums.title: Beaujolais Wine Museums | Cork & Curve
wine-museums.description: Wine museums in Beaujolais including the Hameau Duboeuf theme park at Romanèche-Thorins and the Maison du Terroir Beaujolais in Villefranche.

wine-tours.title: Beaujolais Wine Tours | Cork & Curve
wine-tours.description: Guided wine tours of Beaujolais from Villefranche-sur-Saone through the ten crus of Morgon, Fleurie, Moulin-a-Vent, Chiroubles and Brouilly.

wine-hotels.title: Beaujolais Wine Hotels | Cork & Curve
wine-hotels.description: Vineyard hotels, estate stays and wine-country accommodation in Beaujolais from Chateau de Pizay in Morgon to Brouilly and Fleurie estate B&Bs.

wine-festivals.title: Beaujolais Wine Festivals | Cork & Curve
wine-festivals.description: Annual Beaujolais wine events from the Beaujolais Nouveau release in November to the summer cru festivals of Chiroubles, Fleurie and Moulin-a-Vent.

signature-wines.title: Iconic Beaujolais Wines | Cork & Curve
signature-wines.description: The wines that define Beaujolais, from natural Morgon by Marcel Lapierre and Jean Foillard to Moulin-a-Vent, Fleurie and Cote de Brouilly from Chateau Thivin.

dietary.title: Biodynamic and Natural Beaujolais Wines | Cork & Curve
dietary.description: Natural, organic and biodynamic Beaujolais producers from the Demeter-certified Regnié estates to the Gang of Four natural pioneers of Morgon.

hidden-gems.title: Hidden Gem Beaujolais Estates | Cork & Curve
hidden-gems.description: Lesser-known Beaujolais estates and cru producers that locals love, from small Chenas and Chiroubles domaines to natural Julienas and Regnié producers.

food-pairing.title: Beaujolais Food Pairings | Cork & Curve
food-pairing.description: Classic Beaujolais Gamay food pairings from Lyon bouchon dishes and charcuterie to roast chicken and mushroom risotto. Pair your visit with nearby Lyon.

faqs.q: What is the main grape of Beaujolais?
faqs.a: Gamay Noir a Jus Blanc accounts for over 98 per cent of Beaujolais plantings. Chardonnay is grown for the small Beaujolais Blanc AOC, and Pinot Noir appears in rare crossover blends under the Coteaux Bourguignons AOC.

faqs.q: What are the ten Beaujolais crus?
faqs.a: The ten named crus, each with its own AOC, are Saint-Amour, Julienas, Chenas, Moulin-a-Vent, Fleurie, Chiroubles, Morgon, Regnié, Brouilly and Cote de Brouilly. Their labels carry only the cru name, not the word Beaujolais.

faqs.q: What is the Gang of Four in Beaujolais?
faqs.a: The Gang of Four refers to four Morgon producers whose methods were shaped by scientist Jules Chauvet: Marcel Lapierre, Jean Foillard, Guy Breton and Jean-Paul Thevenet. From the 1980s they pioneered natural winemaking with whole-cluster fermentation, wild yeasts and minimal sulfur, inspiring the global natural-wine movement.

Beaujolais Nouveau is designed to be drunk within months of release. However, wines from Moulin-a-Vent, Morgon and Chenas develop bottle complexity over five to ten years. The best natural Morgon cuvees from Foillard and Lapierre are often at their best after three to eight years.

faqs.q: What is Beaujolais Nouveau and when does it release?
faqs.a: Beaujolais Nouveau is a carbonic-maceration Gamay released on the third Thursday of November each year. Georges Duboeuf popularised global release events from the 1970s. Today it represents a small fraction of production, with serious cru Beaujolais attracting more critical and consumer attention.
Yes. The southernmost Beaujolais vineyards lie roughly 20 kilometres north of Lyon city centre. The local saying holds that Lyon is watered by three rivers: the Rhone, the Saone, and Beaujolais.
